rationale: In the citing paper, Berger and Schwartz (2011) appears only as part of an introductory grouped citation supporting a broad statement that firms seek to influence and understand WOM. The paper does not discuss the cited study's immediate-versus-ongoing WOM framework, does not use it to build hypotheses, and does not rely on its measures or methods. The core theorizing and empirical design are developed from other sources, so this citation functions as background context rather than a meaningful building block.
